content.full_text:
TO: [x] JEANNE   [ ] JAY
[ ] LYNN   [ ] BARBARA
[ ] RICH   [ ] BOB S.
[ ] HELEN   [x] OTHER Mary Ann

FROM: Carla
DATE: 7/2/84
No. T-84-177

[x] TELEPHONE ADVICE - SEE BELOW
[ ] CORRESPONDENCE ADVICE - SEE ATTACHED DRAFT
(IF YOU HAVE ANY COMMENTS, NOTIFY ADVISOR WITHIN 1 DAY)

FILE LOCATION: 86109

INCLUDE IN ADVICE PACKAGE: YES [ ] NO [x]

CALLER Sue
REPRESENTING L.A. Unified School District
PHONE NUMBER: (213) 625-6451

QUESTION: For purposes of determining whether an employee has spent 10% or more of his or her compensated time on lobbying activities, must travel time be counted?

ANSWER: No. Travel time should not be counted. However, if an employee's other compensated time for lobbying reaches 10% or more in a calendar month, travel time should be included in the amount reported on the employer's report for gross compensation of employees.
